commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b9e10a9586df3ec0d9d2ab629302bfba451018e7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR expands Codex OAuth refresh-failure matching and user guidance, rejects stale auto-selected Codex auth profiles, changes legacy OAuth sidecar Keychain handling, and adds auth regression tests plus changelog coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main lacks several current Codex terminal refresh-token patterns, and the PR adds focused coverage for those messages and stale Codex auth-profile selection. A live revoked-token session was not reproduced.
commands.review_followup: Protected MEMBER draft PR needs maintainer review for the legacy Keychain sidecar upgrade tradeoff; this is not a safe automated repair lane without that decision.

target.title: fix(auth): match Codex OAuth refresh failures
target.body:
## Summary

- expand OAuth refresh-failure classification to match current Codex refresh-token reuse, expiry, invalidation, revocation, and account-mismatch messages
- keep generic app-server/account-state refresh drift non-terminal so users are not forced to sign in again without high-confidence evidence
- replace user-facing token/re-auth jargon with plain OpenAI account sign-in guidance in chat replies, doctor output, and status summaries
- tighten Codex app-server auth bridge coverage for persisted `oauthRef`-backed OpenClaw OAuth profiles
- add changelog coverage for the Codex harness auth behavior

## Verification

- `node --import tsx --eval '...'` OAuth copy/classifier smoke: passed
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH="$(mktemp -d)/vitest-fs-cache" OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=90000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-refresh-failure.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.hints.test.ts src/commands/status-all/gateway.test.ts --maxWorkers=1`: blocked by local Vitest hang after an initial assertion failure was fixed
- `CI=true blacksmith testbox run --id tbx_01krjksmrsdr4qw582b1f2rgjp "pnpm check:changed"`: passed
- earlier `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`: blocked on raw AWS box without `pnpm` (`run_f6d9596ffce0`, exit 127), then replaced by Blacksmith Testbox proof above

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex app-server OAuth refresh failures now classify current Codex terminal refresh-token states precisely while user-facing messages explain that OpenClaw cannot use the user's OpenAI account for Codex until they sign in again. Generic account-state drift remains retryable/non-terminal.

Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox `tbx_01krjksmrsdr4qw582b1f2rgjp` on the OpenClaw CI check workflow.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `CI=true blacksmith testbox run --id tbx_01krjksmrsdr4qw582b1f2rgjp "pnpm check:changed"`.

Evidence after fix: changed gate completed successfully after syncing commit `77e5c261008`.

Observed result after fix: OAuth copy/classifier smoke passes locally; `pnpm check:changed` passes remotely with typecheck, lint, import-cycle, and changed-surface checks.

What was not tested: a live Docker session with a real revoked/expired OpenAI refresh token was not reproduced; coverage is source-level parity with current Codex messages plus mocked OAuth refresh paths.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
